title: EDHOC responder rejects valid SUITES_I array format per RFC 9528
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 679-682

The responder only accepts SUITES_I as a single byte:
```rust
let suites_i = msg1[1];
if suites_i != SUITE_0 {
    return Err(EdhocError::UnsupportedSuite);
}
```

Per RFC 9528 Section 3.3.2, SUITES_I can be either:
- A single int (the selected suite)
- An array of ints [selected_suite, ...other_supported_suites]

If initiator sends [0, 2] (prefer Suite 0, also supports Suite 2), msg1[1] would be 0x82 (CBOR array header), not 0x00, causing rejection.

Impact: Interoperability failure with compliant EDHOC implementations that send suite arrays.

Fix: Parse SUITES_I as CBOR and extract the first element if it's an array.
